zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Featter Stare於1553年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可利頓時，他的父親John Stare23歲，母親Agnes Cate20歲。他於1582年5月23日與Agnes Cate在英國英格蘭戴文可利頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1588年在他的家鄉享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文可利頓聖 安德也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Stare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slovenian: from a derivative of star ‘old’, applied as a nickname for an old man or as an identifying name for an ‘old’ inhabitant, as distinct from a newcomer (compare Novak ).
English: nickname from Middle English star(e), ster (Old English stær) ‘starling’.

variants.name: Novak
meanings: Czech, Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Hungarian (Novák); Slovenian, Croatian, Serbian, Ukrainian, Rusyn, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): derivative of Czech and Slovak nový, Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian nov, Ukrainian and Rusyn novy ‘new’, denoting a newcomer to a place and, as a Slovenian surname, also e.g. a peasant who settled on newly cleared land. This is the most frequent surname in Czechia and in Slovenia and the sixth most frequent surname in Croatia. Compare Novack , Nowak 2, and Newman 2.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: Featter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English fether, fed(d)er ‘feather’ or perhaps a shortened form of Middle English fetherer, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a trader in feathers and down, a maker of quilts, or possibly a maker of pens. Feathermongers are recorded from the 13th century onward.

English: perhaps also a variant of Father, from Middle English fader, father, feder, fether (Old English fæder) ‘father’, often used to denote someone who exercised protecting care like that of a father.

Native American: translation into English and shortening of a personal name based on a word, such as Chippewa miigwan or Lakota Sioux wiyaka, meaning ‘feather’. The great cultural significance of the eagle feathers to many Native American tribes is reflected in their traditional personal names, many of which were adopted as surnames (translated into English), e.g. Red Feather (see Redfeather ) and White Feather (see Whitefeather ).

variants.name: Feathers
meanings: Scottish (Fife): habitational name from Fetters in Leuchars.

variants.name: Feder
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Baruch, Aron, Chaim, Meyer, Zev, Arie, Aryeh, Avi.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a trader in feathers (see Feather ) or in quill pens, from Middle High German veder(e), German Feder ‘feather, quill, pen’.

variants.name: Fedder
meanings: North German and Danish: variant of   Vedder  .

variants.name: Redfeather
meanings: Native American (also Red Feather): translation into English of a personal name such as Lakota Sioux Wiyaka Luta, composed of wiyaka ‘feather’ and luta ‘red’. See also Feather 3.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Featter Stare was born in 1553, in Colyton, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Stare, was 23 and his mother, Agnes Cate, was 20. He married Agnes Cate on 23 May 1582, in Colyton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1588, in his hometown, at the age of 35, and was buried in St Andrew's Church, Colyton, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Agnes Cate le 23 mai 1582, à Colyton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1588, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à St Andrew's Church, Colyton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lui sposato Agnes Cate il 23 maggio 1582, nel Colyton, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. ko.Saint Andrew's Church, Colyton, East Dev: 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코리턴 스트 앤드루'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Featter Stare(이)가 1553년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코리턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Stare(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Cate(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1582년 5월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코리턴에서 Agnes Cate(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1588년 그의 고향에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코리턴 스트 앤드루'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Он умер в 1588 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности ст андрю'с чурч, колитон, девон, Англия, Великобритания.
